questions.prompt: What does the text most directly suggest about translation apps?
questions.explanation: The passage states that translation apps are 'increasingly obviating the need to learn new languages' due to 'advances in language processing technology'. However, it also mentions that 'richer communication...often hinges on conversational patterns and gradations of meaning', implying that the apps are not sufficient for complex interactions. Therefore, they have improved but are still insufficient for complex interactions.
questions.passage: Linguist John McWhorter asserts that translation apps for smartphones and computers are-despite generally failing to convey many nuances-increasingly obviating the need to learn new languages. Advances in language processing technology have greatly boosted the utility of these apps for perfunctory tasks, like inquiring about an item on a menu, and passing interactions; be that as it may, richer communication (e.g., in business dealings or meaningful personal exchanges) often hinges on conversational patterns and gradations of meaning.

options.A: They have improved remarkably over time but remain insufficient to support the complexity called for in certain interactions.
options.D: They are coming to be embraced by international tourists but are viewed with skepticism by many business professionals.
options.C: They are becoming simpler to use but are inconsistent in how comprehensively they cover different languages.
options.B: They have gained impressive capabilities but continue to be widely viewed as inadequate for most practical purposes.

options.D: at 1 V, mean adhesion strength was approximately equal to adhesion strength at 0 V.

options.B: at 3 V, mean adhesion strength reached its highest observed level at approximately 30 kPa.

questions.prompt: Which choice most logically completes the text?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the differences between diving behaviors in the two exposure types (ship sounds vs. ship sounds + sonic pulses) were negligible. This suggests that the addition of sonic pulses had little effect, implying that ship sounds already contributed significantly to the overall sound level. Therefore, option D is the most logical completion.
questions.passage: Outi Tervo and team studied the effect of human-caused noise on narwhals (Monodon monoceros), arctic marine mammals that are sensitive to acoustic changes in their environment. Hypothesizing that elevated sound levels affect foraging among narwhals, Tervo’s team compared narwhal diving behaviors in natural sound conditions with those behaviors in two human-caused sound exposure conditions—ship sounds or ship sounds coupled with sonic pulses. Both exposure conditions resulted in significant decreases in the number and target depth of deep dives (associated with foraging) relative to natural conditions. However, differences between diving behaviors in the two exposure types were negligible, a finding that could be attributed to the fact that

options.A: the narwhals weren’t as sensitive to human-caused sounds as the researchers had predicted.
options.D: ship sounds contribute so much to the overall sound level that the addition of sonic pulses has little effect on the narwhals’ auditory environment.
options.C: narwhals forage at shallower depths in the presence of ship sounds alone than in the presence of ship sounds coupled with sonic pulses.
options.B: sonic pulses can be heard at significantly greater ocean depths than ship sounds can.

questions.passage:
• Lighthouses send out crucial light signals to help ships and other watercraft navigate at night.
  • Before automation, lighthouses were run by lighthouse keepers.
  • Fannie May Salter was the lighthouse keeper at Turkey Point Light in Maryland.
  • She held this position for twenty-two years (1925–1947).
  • Ida Lewis was the lighthouse keeper at Lime Rock Light in Rhode Island.
  • She held this position for fifty-four years (1857–1911).

options.A: Fannie May Salter and Ida Lewis were both lighthouse keepers at a time when such a person was needed to operate a lighthouse.
options.D: Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Fannie May Salter, who served for twenty-two years, and Ida Lewis were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.
options.C: Fannie May Salter worked as a lighthouse keeper for twenty-two years, but Ida Lewis did so for even longer, fifty-four years.
options.B: From 1925 to 1947, the nighttime waters of Maryland were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Fannie May Salter and Ida Lewis.

questions.prompt: Which choice best states the main purpose of the text?
questions.explanation: The text introduces the topic of juvenile sea turtle migration, notes that it is understudied, and then summarizes a research study aimed at learning more about this stage. Therefore, the main purpose is to summarize research on an understudied stage of sea turtle development.
questions.passage: Understanding juvenile sea turtles' migratory patterns is crucial for conservation efforts. While adult sea turtles are well studied, little is known about juveniles during their oceanic stage. When they spend most of their time in the open ocean. To learn about this stage, researchers tagged and released six juvenile green sea turtles (Chelonia mydas) into the eastern Caribbean Sea. Their findings revealed that the majority of the turtles passively drifted with ocean currents throughout the region. However, two individuals swam against the currents, possibly in search of foraging grounds off the coast of South America.

options.A: To compare the behavior of adult and juvenile sea turtles during migration
options.B: To summarize research on an understudied stage of sea turtle development

questions.prompt: Which choice most logically completes the text?
questions.explanation: The passage states that ferritic stainless steel (used for induction cookers) contains a higher percentage of chromium than austenitic stainless steel (used for home water tanks). Therefore, home water tanks will have a lower percentage of chromium than stainless steel used for induction cookers.
questions.passage: All stainless steel contains varying amounts of iron, carbon, and corrosion-inhibiting chromium. However, ferritic stainless steel, often used for induction cookers, contains a higher percentage of chromium (at least 10.5%) than does austenitic stainless steel and a higher concentration of iron, which is responsible for its magnetic properties. Unlike ferritic stainless steel, austenitic stainless steel has a face-centered cubic crystalline structure resulting from the addition of nickel to the alloy. Austenitic stainless steel has two subtypes: the 200 series, often used for home water tanks, and the 300 series, which has more nickel than the 200 series and is often used for storage containers or sinks. Thus, stainless steel used to manufacture

options.A: induction cookers will have a face-centered cubic crystalline structure.
options.D: sinks will have similar magnetic properties to stainless steel used to manufacture induction cookers.
options.C: storage containers will have a concentration of chromium lower than 10.5%, while stainless steel used to manufacture home water tanks will not.
options.B: home water tanks will have a lower percentage of chromium in its composition than stainless steel used for induction cookers will.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?

"Prescient" means having or showing knowledge of events before they take place, which fits the context of the sentence, suggesting that Toffler's claim was remarkably accurate or insightful.
questions.passage: If some specific predictions in Alvin Toffler’s 1970 book Future Shock seem ludicrous now-people do not wear paper clothing while living in underwater cities-Toffler’s fundamental claim that rapid technological and social change will leave people feeling disoriented and atomized seems, in our age of disequilibrium and fragmentation, remarkably ________.

Aunt Polly asked [Tom] questions that were full of guile, and very deep-for she wanted to trap him into damaging revealments. options.A: To convey Aunt Polly’s perception of herself as clever and astute
options.D: To describe the setting in which Aunt Polly and Tom are having dinner
options.C: To illustrate Tom’s tendency to be disobedient and deceptive

questions.prompt: Which statement about the use of hydrogen in the steel and heating industries is most strongly supported by the text?
questions.explanation: The question asks which statement about the use of hydrogen in the steel and heating industries is most strongly supported by the text. The correct answer is D because the passage states that there is utility in using hydrogen to decarbonize the steel and heating industries, where reducing carbon emissions is especially challenging.
questions.passage: From steel production to fuel for residential heating, hydrogen has many applications. Currently, producing hydrogen is carbon intensive, but Rebecca R. Beswick is one of many scholars investigating the feasibility of large-scale production of hydrogen through electrolysis, a process that emits no carbon when renewable energy is used. Beswick suggests that although some obstacles may prevent electrolytic hydrogen from dominating energy systems, there is utility in using it to decarbonize the steel and heating industries, where reducing carbon emissions is especially challenging.

options.A: Development of applications for electrolytic hydrogen is less advanced in these industries than it is in most other carbon-intensive industries.
options.D: At least some of electrolytic hydrogen's potential to lessen these industries' carbon emissions is as yet unrealized.
options.C: The adoption of electrolytic hydrogen by these industries has been impeded by concerns about how carbon intensive its production is.
options.B: The fact that large-scale electrolytic hydrogen production is not yet available has limited these industries' interest in pursuing hydrogen-based applications.

questions.prompt: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole?
questions.explanation: The question asks which choice best describes the function of the underlined sentence in the text as a whole. The correct answer is D because the sentence proposes that the distinction made between present-day nonisolate languages and isolates like Zuni and Haida may not have been applicable in the distant past.
questions.passage: Most Native languages belong to language families, or groups of languages whose structural and lexical correspondence likely derives from their descent from a single language spoken long ago. A minority, such as Zuni, which is spoken in New Mexico, and Haida, which is spoken in Alaska, are isolates, having no demonstrable genealogical relationship to other languages. Yet Zuni and Haida, like all isolates, are potentially remnants of families whose other members vanished before the historical record could attest to them, perhaps through the geographical expansion of extant families.

options.A: It implies that a nonisolate language may have influenced both Zuni and Haida to such a degree that their relationship to their former families is no longer discernible.
options.D: It proposes that the distinction made between present-day nonisolate languages and isolates like Zuni and Haida may not have been applicable in the distant past.
options.C: It suggests that the ancestral languages of both Zuni and Haida were likely isolates that replaced nonisolate languages through geographic expansion in the distant past.
options.B: It asserts that the historical record tends to overrepresent nonisolate languages relative to isolates, such that the origins of Zuni and Haida are obscure to scholars in the present day.

questions.prompt: Which choice most logically completes the text?
questions.explanation: The question asks which choice most logically completes the text. The correct answer is A because the passage states that the ecotypes grew equally well in all four water samples and that adding zinc consistently enhanced growth, regardless of concentration, suggesting that if each ecotype is indeed locally adapted as the researchers hypothesized, those adaptations are to other environmental conditions than the water each ecotype inhabits.
questions.passage: Duckweed is a small freshwater plant that is often exposed to zinc pollution. Sofia Vamos and colleagues collected samples of four duckweed ecotypes (genetically and geographically distinct populations within a species), along with water from each ecotype's habitat. Hypothesizing that each ecotype is adapted to its local conditions in ways that bolster its growth and resistance to pollutants, the researchers grew each ecotype in all four water samples and with three levels of zinc (none, low, high). (The researchers did not replicate local differences in light or temperature.) They found that the ecotypes grew equally well in all four water samples and that adding zinc consistently enhanced growth, regardless of concentration, suggesting that

options.A: if each ecotype is indeed locally adapted as the researchers hypothesized, those adaptations are to other environmental conditions than the water each ecotype inhabits.
options.D: while the ecotypes are genetically and geographically distinct, those differences do not represent adaptations to local environmental conditions.
options.C: although the researchers' hypothesis does not appear to be supported, this may be because the levels of zinc exposure the plants in the experiment received did not match their exposure in their natural environments.
options.B: there may not be significant differences in the water that each ecotype inhabits, but there are significant differences in each ecotype's resistance to zinc pollution.

questions.prompt: Based on the text, what would be the most reasonable next step for the students to take to accomplish their goal?
questions.explanation: The question asks what would be the most reasonable next step for the students to take to accomplish their goal. The correct answer is A because the students need to compare Oklahoma to a similar state without RSRs to see if the RSRs had an effect.
questions.passage: In 2001, the US state of Oklahoma enacted rate stability regulations (RSRs), constraining insurance companies' latitude to raise premiums (the recurring fees policyholders pay to maintain insurance policies) once policies are in effect. Although RSRs are intended to benefit consumers, Nael Alziq and Ami Ko note that RSRs could curtail insurers' profits to such a degree that insurers abandon the market, thereby reducing the competitive pressure that typically restrains premium prices for newly issued policies. To determine whether this occurred in Oklahoma, students first collect data on the number of insurers in the state for a few years leading up to and following 2001 and the premium prices for new policies offered by those insurers.

options.A: Compare changes over time within each of the two types of data the students have collected with changes over time in analogous data for the same period from an otherwise similar state that had not enacted RSRs
options.C: Compare changes over time within each of the two types of data the students have collected with changes over time in the same types of data from Oklahoma for a period beginning several years after 2001
options.B: Compare changes over time in the insurer-number data the students have collected with changes over time in insurer-number data from another state that enacted RSRs but not during the same period

questions.explanation: The sentence contrasts the Yupno people's lack of artificial time-tracking methods with the fact that their language contains temporal expressions. "Dependent on" fits the context, suggesting that the Yupno people are not dependent on artificial means of tracking time because their language provides temporal references.
questions.passage: The Yupno people of the remote Finisterre Range of Papua New Guinea do not measure age and have no artificial means (e.g., clocks) of tracking time, but this does not imply that the Yupno are time. As Rafael Núñez and colleagues have recently shown, the Yupno language contains many temporal expressions, such as usa-don sonda, which translates to “next week.”

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation: The word "conversely" indicates a contrast between the actors' brain activity during performances and in nonacting contexts. This contrast supports the idea that actors temporarily set aside their personal identities when embodying characters.
questions.passage: Brain imaging research led by neuroscientist Dwayne Greaves found that actors showed suppressed responses in the left anterior prefrontal cortex (the portion of the brain associated with self-awareness) when their names were called during performances; ________ the actors’ responses were normal in nonacting contexts. These findings suggest that when embodying characters, performers may temporarily set aside their personal identities.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation: The word "still" indicates that despite the fact that the rooftop couldn't grow in a forest, one can still see natural influences in the architect's designs. This shows that the architect still championed nature in their designs.
questions.passage: Nineteenth-century Modernista architects championed nature in their designs. Granted, the domed rooftop and painted floral designs of Casa Estapé, a Modernista private home designed by Bernardí Martorell i Rius, couldn’t exactly grow in a forest, ________ one sees natural influences in Martorell i Rius’s penchant for curves (rather than right angles) and plant- and animal-inspired flourishes.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
questions.explanation: The word "hence" indicates that the inability of the 2011 Mars Curiosity rover to preserve samples for future analysis led to the creation of a mechanism that could do exactly that in the 2020 Mars Perseverance rover. This shows that the 2020 Mars Perseverance rover was created as a result of the 2011 Mars Curiosity rover's limitations.
questions.passage: Though its onboard laboratory allowed it to analyze rock samples on-site, the 2011 Mars Curiosity rover was unable to preserve samples for future analysis. ________ when creating the 2020 Mars Perseverance rover, electrical engineer Dania Lazo and other members of NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory sought to implement a mechanism that could do exactly that.

questions.prompt: The student wants to explain the significance of Minnesota’s NPVIC adoption. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
questions.explanation: The question asks for the significance of Minnesota's adoption of the NPVIC. Option C accurately reflects the notes by stating that if the NPVIC takes effect, Minnesota will award its electoral votes to the winner of the national popular vote, rather than the state's popular vote.
questions.passage:
• In the Electoral College system, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The winner of Minnesota’s popular vote determines the outcome of US presidential elections, the winner of Minnesota’s popular vote receives all 10 of Minnesota’s electoral votes.
  • A 2001 article written by law professors Akhil and Vikram Amar proposed that states could instead agree to award their electoral votes to the winner of the national popular vote.
  • The Amars’ idea is the basis of the National Popular Vote Interstate Compact (NPVIC).
  • If states holding a majority of electoral college votes (270) adopt the NPVIC, the compact will take effect and adopters will award their electoral votes accordingly.
  • Minnesota adopted the NPVIC in 2023.

options.A: Because it has adopted the NPVIC, Minnesota will award its electoral votes to the winner of the state’s popular vote-once states holding a majority of electoral college votes (270) do the same.
options.D: An agreement among US states to award their electoral votes to the presidential candidate who wins the national popular vote was adopted by Minnesota in 2023.
options.C: If the NPVIC takes effect, adopter Minnesota will award all 10 of its electoral votes to the winner of the national rather than the state’s, popular vote.
options.B: According to the idea proposed by the Amars, the winner of Minnesota’s popular vote will receive all of the state’s electoral votes if the NPVIC takes effect.

questions.prompt: The words in a blocky digital font in Rainbow Road reflect Cody's childhood familiarity with blocky video game graphics-what she calls "this world of pixelization."
questions.explanation: The prompt asks for a quotation that connects to a nontraditional design element. Option A directly quotes Cody referring to "this world of pixelization," which is a nontraditional design element related to video games.
questions.passage: The student wants to connect a quotation to a particular nontraditional design element in Cody's work. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

options.A: The words in a blocky digital font in Rainbow Road reflect Cody's childhood familiarity with blocky video game graphics-what she calls "this world of pixelization."
options.D: According to Cody, some elements of Lightning Storm, such as a blue zigzag stripe, "weren't considered tradition when they first came about."
options.C: Cody's tapestry Rainbow Road featuresa pattern of colored diamonds, a traditional design element that "we consider tradition now."
options.B: Cody's background as "a child of '80s video game culture" factored into her decision to blend traditional and nontraditional design elements in Lightning Storm.

questions.prompt: The student wants to make and support a claim regarding the applicability of the farm-size transition hypothesis to Thailand. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?
questions.explanation: The prompt asks for a claim regarding the applicability of the farm-size transition hypothesis to Thailand. Option B directly addresses this by stating that the predicted shift to large-scale farming may not hold true for rice farms in Thailand, supported by Promkhambut et al.'s findings.
questions.passage:
• The farm-size transition hypothesis predicts that economic pressures associated with modernization result in smaller farms amalgamating into larger-scale commercial farms.
  • Masters et al. (2013): The average farm size in Asia "already has or will soon begin to rise."
  • Promkhambut et al. (2023) argue that small rice farms in Thailand have adopted modern farming methods without a significant scaling up of farm size.
  • Promkhambut et al.: "The persistence of [small] rice farms [in Thailand] does not represent a failure to modernize, or a truncated transition; it is a response to modernization."

options.A: Masters et al. report that the average farm size "already has or will soon begin to rise" in Asia, a finding that is consistent with the farm-size transition hypothesis.
options.D: Although the farm-size transition hypothesis may be applicable to some countries in Asia, it is inconsistent with the development of rice farming in Thailand.
options.C: Taken together, the studies by Masters et al. and Promkhambut et al. suggest that rice farms in Thailand have responded to the economic pressures associated with modernization by expanding in size.
options.B: The predicted shift to large-scale, commercial farming may not hold true for rice farms in Thailand, where, according to Promkhambut et al., rice farms have remained small as they've modernized.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
questions.explanation: The sentence describes the relationship between marine structures and ocean forces, where the continuous impact of waves necessitates reinforcements or redesigns. "Interplay" fits the context, suggesting a dynamic interaction between the structures and the ocean forces, where each influences the other.
questions.passage: Exposed to harsh environmental conditions for centuries, lighthouses such as La Jument lighthouse off the coast of Brittany, France, exemplify the ________ between marine structures and ocean forces: the continuous impact of breaking waves necessitates periodic reinforcements to or redesigns of the structures that in turn may alter local wave patterns.

questions.prompt: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
questions.explanation: The passage begins by introducing the widely held belief that PAs hinder economic development. It then suggests that this belief is prevalent because economic assessments often don't capture the indirect effects of tourism. Finally, it details a study by Håkan Bergamini et al. whose findings seemingly conflict with the initial belief.
questions.passage: Establishing protected areas (PAs), such as South Luangwa National Park in Zambia, is a common conservation strategy, but because PAs restrict some kinds of economic activity, it’s widely thought that they hinder economic development. This perception is driven in part by the fact that economic assessments often don’t capture the indirect effects of tourism linked to PAs, Håkan Bergamini et al. found that tourism associated with South Luangwa boosts local demand for goods and services in other economic sectors, resulting in, for instance, increased income from livestock production in nearby areas.

options.A: It makes a generalization about the difficulty of using PAs to reconcile economic and conservation priorities, demonstrates why most PAs fall short of achieving that goal, and then holds up a particular PA as an example of how that goal can be achieved.
options.D: It states a hypothesis that accounts for why PAs generally negatively impact local economies, outlines how a research team’s findings support that hypothesis, and then suggests how a novel methodology led a separate team of researchers to a different conclusion.
options.C: It introduces a widely held belief about the effect PAs have on the economy, proposes a reason for that belief’s prevalence, and then details a study whose findings seemingly conflict with that belief.
options.B: It explains how PAs typically affect economic development in the regions where they are established, summarizes the findings of several economic studies that support this explanation, and then concedes that a particular PA is an exception to the general trend.

questions.prompt: Which choice most logically completes the text?
questions.explanation: The passage describes how Picasso subverted the norms of traditional painting and defied conventional grammar in his poetry. This suggests that both his paintings and poems are linked by his prioritization of creative expression over the standard rules of the art forms.
questions.passage: Pablo Picasso famously subverted the norms of traditional painting: in his cubist paintings he refused to let his expression be constrained, fragmenting objects and figures to present multiple perspectives simultaneously. Though less widely known, Picasso-who once lamented that writers of his time had “limited themselves to moving around words somewhat while respecting the syntax”-also wrote poetry that defied conventional grammar, semantic relationships, and text structure. Thus, the paintings and poems are linked in that ________.

options.A: the poems are intended to be understood as explanations of the artistic inclinations reflected in the paintings.
options.D: both types of work are characterized by the simultaneous representation of multiple points of view that Picasso is known for.
options.C: both exhibit Picasso’s prioritization of creative expression over the standard rules of the art forms.
options.B: the poems present many of the same subjects as the paintings but with different thematic approaches.

questions.prompt: Which finding, if true, would most directly support the idea that social buffering occurs among C. helleri?
questions.explanation: The prompt asks for evidence of social buffering, which is when the presence of a companion reduces stress. Option D directly supports this idea by stating that the average percent change in heart rate (an indicator of stress) was lower when C. helleri had a companion.
questions.passage: companion C. helleri. The researchers compared the percent change between baseline and peak heart rate in response to a (harmless) disturbance, with higher values indicating higher stress levels.

options.A: Solitary C. helleri had higher average baseline heart rates than did C. helleri in the other treatments, but the average percent change in heart rate was similar among solitary C. helleri than among C. helleri with a companion.
options.D: The average percent change in heart rate was lower among C. helleri with a companion than among solitary C. helleri and C. helleri with a rope.
options.C: C. helleri with a companion displayed a lower average baseline heart rate and lower average peak heart rate than did solitary C. helleri or C. helleri with a rope.
options.B: Average peak heart rates were highest among solitary C. helleri, but no differences were observed in average peak heart rates between C. helleri with a companion and C. helleri with a rope.

questions.prompt: Which finding, if true, would most directly weaken Carter and Kilikoglou’s argument?
questions.explanation: Carter and Kilikoglou argue that consistent obsidian-object production methods indicate local cultural continuity. If the obsidian-object production method was less efficient than other methods, it would suggest that the culture was not continuous, but rather was influenced by other cultures. Therefore, option B weakens their argument.
questions.passage: Variously, researchers have closely examined obsidian artifacts to understand ancient social and economic structures, as in Raymond V. Sidrys’s 1976 study, or to glean aspects of cultural identity, as in M. Steven Shackley’s 2002 study. Studies of the Malia archaeological site on the Mediterranean island of Crete have shown that significant changes to building styles/changes coincided with an influx of people from another culture elsewhere in the Mediterranean and occurred during the Middle Bronze Age to the Late Bronze Age. In a 2022 study, Tristan Carter and Vassilis Kilikoglou found that obsidian-object production methods at Malia stayed remarkably consistent during this architectural transition, which they interpret as indicative of local cultural continuity in the Middle and Late Bronze Ages.

options.A: The obsidian used to produce objects at Malia was transported to Crete from the same source elsewhere in the Mediterranean throughout the Middle and Late Bronze Ages.
options.D: Changes to buildings like those that occurred at Malia have not been linked to changes in obsidian-object production methods in other Mediterranean cultures during the Middle and Late Bronze Ages.
options.C: The methods used to produce obsidian objects at Malia during the Middle and Late Bronze Ages were also used by some other Mediterranean cultures in the period.
options.B: The obsidian-object production method that was most common among other Mediterranean cultures during the Middle and Late Bronze Ages was more efficient than the method used at Malia.

questions.prompt: Based on the texts, how would the critics mentioned in Text 2 most likely respond to the underlined claim in Text 1?
questions.explanation: The underlined claim in Text 1 suggests that public art provokes backlash if it fails to appease everyone. Text 2 states that norm-defying pieces that aren't effectively integrated within their surroundings tend to be regarded more unfavorably. Thus, the critics in Text 2 would likely contend that the reactions to controversial public artworks aren't exclusively the result of the attributes inherent in the works themselves, but also the context in which they are displayed.
questions.passage:
Text 1 Good art often challenges and disrupts social and aesthetic norms, but the creation of public artpaintings, sculptures, and performance pieces displayed in nonmuseum or nontheatrical public settings -typically requires broad agreement among artists, civic officials, and community members about the works’ message and artistic goals. Public art that fails to appease everyone by being sufficiently aesthetically and conceptually bland almost inevitably provokes backlash.

Text 2 Public art is commonly displayed in spaces intended for purposes other than meaningfui aesthetic engagement. Some critics of public art therefore note that norm-defying pieces that aren’t effectively integrated within their surroundings in a manner that primes passersby to appreciate the pieces’ merits (as is often the case) tend to be regarded more unfavorably than similarly provocative art encountered in museums is.

options.A: By arguing that the reason members of the general public might disagree about a public artwork’s merits is unrelated to the unconventionality of its appearance and ideas
options.D: By contending that the kinds of reactions controversial public artworks often receive aren’t exclusively the result of attributes inherent in the works themselves
options.C: By agreeing with the idea that only works of art that are universally appealing are suitable for displaying in public spaces
options.B: By disputing the notion that civic leaders and community members are easily placated by art that is intended mainly to reinforce social norms

questions.prompt: Which choice best describes the overall structure of the text?
questions.explanation: The passage introduces Tomi Adeyemi's debut novel, contextualizes it within the Afrofuturism literary tradition, and then describes the important themes of identity and power within that tradition. This aligns with the structure described in option D.
questions.passage: Tomi Adeyemi’s first published work, the inventive West African-inspired fantasy novel Children of Blood and Bone, has intriguing things to say about the relationship between identity and power. Adeyemi works in a decades-long tradition, going back to writers such as Samuel Delany and Octavia Butler, who wove cultural elements of the Black diaspora into their science fiction and fantasy in a movement known as Afrofuturism. At its core, the movement is characterized by speculation, not just about distant futures or other planets but also about alternate versions of our shared past. Afrofuturism’s meditations on authority reinvigorate the creative potential of fiction.

options.A: It praises a newly published author, compares that author with others who might appear superficially similar, and then points out the features of that author’s work that make it unique.
options.D: It introduces an intriguing debut work, contextualizes that work as part of an ongoing literary tradition, and then describes important themes of that tradition.
options.C: It summarizes a book, describes characteristics of the artistic movement of which the book is a part, and then points out the features the book shares with more widely read works.
options.B: It notes the political stance of an author, compares that author’s book to other earlier works, and then analyzes the influence of political events on the book.

The equation ﻿54 = 2x + 2y﻿ gives the perimeter of a rectangular garden that has length ﻿x﻿, in feet, and width ﻿y﻿, in feet. The width of the garden is 12 feet. What is the length, in feet, of the garden?
questions.explanation: Given the equation ﻿54 = 2x + 2y﻿, where ﻿x﻿ is the length and ﻿y﻿ is the width of the rectangular garden. We are given that the width ﻿y = 12﻿ feet. We need to find the length ﻿x﻿. Substitute ﻿y = 12﻿ into the equation: ﻿54 = 2x + 2(12)﻿ ﻿54 = 2x + 24﻿ Subtract 24 from both sides: ﻿54 - 24 = 2x﻿ ﻿30 = 2x﻿ Divide by 2: ﻿\frac{30}{2}﻿">﻿x = ﻿\frac{30}{2}﻿﻿ ﻿x = 15﻿ The length of the garden is 15 feet.

questions.prompt: A beekeeper's initial observation of the population of a certain bee colony was 1,900 bees. The beekeeper set a goal to increase the population to 3,025 bees. The beekeeper uses a model that predicts the population of this bee colony begins at 1,900 and increases by 90 bees per week in the first two weeks after the initial observation, and then increases by 135 bees per week until the beekeeper's goal is reached. According to this model, at the end of week ﻿w﻿ after the initial observation, where ﻿w > 2﻿, which of the following functions gives the predicted number of bees still needed to reach the beekeeper's goal?

In 2012, Chloe earned 17% more than in 2011, and in 2013 Chloe earned 3% more than in 2012. If Chloe earned ﻿y﻿ times as much in 2011 as in 2013, which of the following is closest to the value of ﻿y﻿?
questions.explanation: Let ﻿x﻿ be the amount Chloe earned in 2011. In 2012, she earned ﻿1.17x﻿. In 2013, she earned ﻿1.03(1.17x) = 1.2051x﻿. We are given that Chloe earned ﻿y﻿ times as much in 2011 as in 2013. So, ﻿yx = 1.2051x﻿. Dividing both sides by ﻿x﻿, we get ﻿y = 1.2051﻿. We want to find the value of ﻿1/y﻿, which is ﻿1/1.2051 \approx 0.8298﻿.

questions.prompt: If ﻿\frac{9}{41}﻿">﻿\\cos w^\\circ = \﻿\frac{9}{41}﻿﻿, what is the value of ﻿\ an z^\\circ﻿?
questions.explanation: Since ﻿\\angle ADB﻿ is a right angle, ﻿w + z = 90﻿. Therefore, ﻿z = 90 - w﻿. We want to find ﻿\ an z = \ an(90 - w)﻿. We know that ﻿\frac{1}{\ an w}﻿">﻿\ an(90 - w) = \\cot w = \﻿\frac{1}{\ an w}﻿﻿. Since ﻿\frac{9}{41}﻿">﻿\\cos w = \﻿\frac{9}{41}﻿﻿, we can draw a right triangle with adjacent side 9 and hypotenuse 41. The opposite side is ﻿\sqrt{41^2 - 9^2}﻿ = \﻿\sqrt{1681 - 81}﻿ = \﻿\sqrt{1600}﻿ = 40">﻿\﻿\sqrt{41^2 - 9^2}﻿ = \﻿\sqrt{1681 - 81}﻿ = \﻿\sqrt{1600}﻿ = 40﻿. Therefore, ﻿\frac{40}{9}﻿">﻿\ an w = \﻿\frac{40}{9}﻿﻿, and ﻿\frac{9}{40}﻿">﻿\\cot w = \﻿\frac{9}{40}﻿﻿. So, ﻿\frac{9}{40}﻿">﻿\ an z = \﻿\frac{9}{40}﻿﻿.

questions.prompt: The graph of the function ﻿f﻿, where ﻿y = f(x)﻿, gives the total cost ﻿y﻿, in dollars, for a certain video game system and ﻿x﻿ games. What is the best interpretation of the slope of the graph in this context?
Which of the following expressions is(are) a factor of ﻿3x^2 + 10x - 48﻿? I. ﻿x - 6﻿ II. ﻿3x - 8﻿
questions.explanation: We can factor the quadratic expression ﻿3x^2 + 10x - 48﻿. We are looking for two numbers that multiply to ﻿3(-48) = -144﻿ and add to ﻿10﻿. These numbers are ﻿18﻿ and ﻿-8﻿. So we can rewrite the expression as ﻿3x^2 + 18x - 8x - 48﻿. Factoring by grouping, we get ﻿3x(x + 6) - 8(x + 6) = (3x - 8)(x + 6)﻿. Therefore, ﻿3x - 8﻿ is a factor of ﻿3x^2 + 10x - 48﻿, but ﻿x - 6﻿ is not. Thus, only II is a factor.

questions.prompt: Zuri has a goal to run at least 12 miles per week while training for a race. This week, she has run 8 miles. If ﻿x﻿ represents the additional number of miles Zuri needs to run this week to meet her goal, which inequality represents this situation?
questions.explanation: Zuri has already run 8 miles. She needs to run an additional ﻿x﻿ miles. The total number of miles she runs this week is ﻿8 + x﻿. She wants to run at least 12 miles, which means ﻿8 + x﻿ must be greater than or equal to 12. Therefore, the inequality is ﻿8 + x \\ge 12﻿.
questions.skill: Linear inequalities in one or two variables

questions.prompt: In the ﻿xy﻿-plane, the graph of ﻿y = 6x﻿ intersects the graph of ﻿y = x^2 - 55﻿ at two points. What is the sum of the ﻿x﻿-coordinates of the two points of intersection?
questions.explanation: To find the points of intersection, we set the two equations equal to each other: ﻿6x = x^2 - 55﻿. Rearranging the equation, we get ﻿x^2 - 6x - 55 = 0﻿. Let ﻿x_1﻿ and ﻿x_2﻿ be the two ﻿x﻿-coordinates of the intersection points. We want to find ﻿x_1 + x_2﻿. By Vieta's formulas, the sum of the roots of a quadratic equation ﻿ax^2 + bx + c = 0﻿ is ﻿\frac{b}{a}﻿">﻿-\﻿\frac{b}{a}﻿﻿. In this case, ﻿a = 1﻿, ﻿b = -6﻿, and ﻿c = -55﻿. Therefore, the sum of the roots is ﻿\frac{-6}{1}﻿ = 6">﻿-\﻿\frac{-6}{1}﻿ = 6﻿.
questions.skill: Nonlinear equations in one variable and systems of equations in two variables

Given the slope ﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿">﻿m = \﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿﻿ and the point ﻿(15, -9)﻿, we have: ﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿(x - 15)">﻿y - (-9) = \﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿(x - 15)﻿ ﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 3">﻿y + 9 = \﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 3﻿ ﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 3 - 9">﻿y = \﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 3 - 9﻿ ﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 12">﻿y = \﻿\frac{1}{5}﻿x - 12﻿ Therefore, the equation of the line is ﻿\frac{x}{5}﻿ - 12">﻿y = \﻿\frac{x}{5}﻿ - 12﻿.

The equation ﻿54 = 2x + 2y﻿ gives the perimeter of a rectangular garden that has length ﻿x﻿, in feet, and width ﻿y﻿, in feet. The width of the garden is 12 feet. What is the length, in feet, of the garden?
questions.explanation: Given the equation ﻿54 = 2x + 2y﻿, where ﻿x﻿ is the length and ﻿y﻿ is the width. We are given that the width ﻿y = 12﻿ feet. Substitute ﻿y = 12﻿ into the equation: ﻿54 = 2x + 2(12)﻿. ﻿54 = 2x + 24﻿ Subtract 24 from both sides: ﻿54 - 24 = 2x﻿ ﻿30 = 2x﻿ Divide by 2: ﻿\frac{30}{2}﻿">﻿x = ﻿\frac{30}{2}﻿﻿ ﻿x = 15﻿ The length of the garden is 15 feet.

questions.prompt: A beekeeper's initial observation of the population of a certain bee colony was 1,900 bees. The beekeeper set a goal to increase the population to 3,025 bees. The beekeeper uses a model that predicts the population of this bee colony begins at 1,900 and increases by 90 bees per week in the first two weeks after the initial observation, and then increases by 135 bees per week until the beekeeper's goal is reached. According to this model, at the end of week ﻿w﻿ after the initial observation, where ﻿w > 2﻿, which of the following functions gives the predicted number of bees still needed to reach the beekeeper's goal?
questions.explanation: The beekeeper wants to reach 3,025 bees. The population increases by 135 bees per week after the initial two weeks. So, the number of bees still needed is the goal (3,025) minus the increase of 135 bees per week (﻿135w﻿). Therefore, the function is ﻿p(w) = 3,025 - 135w﻿.

questions.prompt: In 2012, Chloe earned 17% more than in 2011, and in 2013 Chloe earned 3% more than in 2012. If Chloe earned ﻿y﻿ times as much in 2011 as in 2013, which of the following is closest to the value of ﻿y﻿?

The functions ﻿f﻿, ﻿g﻿, and ﻿h﻿ are defined as shown. If ﻿f(x) \cdot g(x) - h(x) = ax^2 + bx + c﻿, where ﻿a﻿, ﻿b﻿, and ﻿c﻿ are constants, what is the value of ﻿b﻿? ﻿f(x) = 2x + 5﻿ ﻿g(x) = 5x - 4﻿ ﻿h(x) = 2x + 3﻿
questions.explanation: First, find ﻿f(x) \cdot g(x)﻿: ﻿(2x + 5)(5x - 4) = 10x^2 - 8x + 25x - 20 = 10x^2 + 17x - 20﻿ Then, subtract ﻿h(x)﻿: ﻿(10x^2 + 17x - 20) - (2x + 3) = 10x^2 + 17x - 20 - 2x - 3 = 10x^2 + 15x - 23﻿ Since ﻿f(x) \cdot g(x) - h(x) = ax^2 + bx + c﻿, we have ﻿10x^2 + 15x - 23 = ax^2 + bx + c﻿. Therefore, ﻿a = 10﻿, ﻿b = 15﻿, and ﻿c = -23﻿. The value of ﻿b﻿ is 15.

questions.prompt: The graph of the function ﻿f﻿, where ﻿y = f(x)﻿, gives the total cost ﻿y﻿, in dollars, for a certain video game system and ﻿x﻿ games. What is the best interpretation of the slope of the graph in this context?

For two acute angles ﻿X﻿ and ﻿Y﻿, the measure of angle ﻿Y﻿ is ﻿28°﻿ and ﻿\frac{\\sin X}{\\sin Y}﻿ = 1">﻿\﻿\frac{\\sin X}{\\sin Y}﻿ = 1﻿. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle ﻿X﻿?
questions.explanation: Since ﻿\frac{\\sin X}{\\sin Y}﻿ = 1">﻿\﻿\frac{\\sin X}{\\sin Y}﻿ = 1﻿, we have ﻿\\sin X = \\sin Y﻿. Since ﻿Y = 28°﻿, we have ﻿\\sin X = \\sin 28°﻿. Since ﻿X﻿ and ﻿Y﻿ are acute angles, ﻿X = Y = 28°﻿.

questions.prompt: A survey was conducted in Arlington to estimate the percent of adult residents who support the addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in their town. Based on the survey, it is estimated that 51% of all adult residents of Arlington support the addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in their town, with an associated margin of error of 7%. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion about all adult residents of Arlington?

options.A: The addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in the town is supported by more than 58% of all adult residents of Arlington.
options.D: The addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in the town is supported by exactly 44% of all adult residents of Arlington.
options.C: Plausible values for the percent of all adult residents of Arlington who support the addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in their town are either less than 44% or more than 58%.
options.B: Plausible values for the percent of all adult residents of Arlington who support the addition of a gas station at a popular intersection in their town are between 44% and 58%.

In triangle ﻿RST﻿, the measure of angle ﻿R﻿ is ﻿49^\\circ﻿, the measure of angle ﻿S﻿ is ﻿x^\\circ﻿, and the measure of angle ﻿T﻿ is ﻿(7x-5)^\\circ﻿. Point ﻿L﻿ lies on ﻿\\overline{RS}﻿, point ﻿K﻿ lies on ﻿\\overline{ST}﻿, and ﻿\\overline{LK}﻿ is parallel to ﻿\\overline{RT}﻿. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle ﻿SKL﻿?
Which point ﻿(x, y)﻿ is a solution to the given system of inequalities in the xy-plane? ﻿y \\ge x + 9﻿ ﻿y \\le -8x - 6﻿
A circle in the xy-plane has its center at ﻿(3, 5)﻿ and has a radius of length 7. Which equation represents this circle?
questions.explanation: The equation of a circle with center ﻿(h, k)﻿ and radius ﻿r﻿ is ﻿(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2﻿. In this case, the center is ﻿(3, 5)﻿ and the radius is 7, so the equation is ﻿(x - 3)^2 + (y - 5)^2 = 7^2 = 49﻿.
